evals.prompt: I want to add SparkScan to my .NET MAUI app. Here's my empty page: EmptyMainPage.xaml + EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, updates MauiProgram.cs with .UseScanditCore().UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddSparkScanView()), adds <scandit:SparkScanView> to MainPage.xaml with the correct namespace and three bindable properties (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanViewSettings), writes complete SparkScan integration into MainPage.xaml.cs (new SparkScan(settings), BarcodeScanned event, ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing forwarded to the SparkScan control, Permissions.Camera). Shows the setup checklist (four NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription on iOS, SupportedOSPlatformVersion ≥ 24 for Android, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditCore()
MauiProgram.cs UseScanditCore call does NOT receive a configure lambda (i.e. no AddDataCaptureView() inside it) for the SparkScan-only case
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditBarcode( with a configure lambda
The UseScanditBarcode configure lambda invokes AddSparkScanView() (not AddDataCaptureView, which is on UseScanditCore for BarcodeCapture, and not AddBarcodeCaptureView)
MainPage.xaml adds the xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Spark.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui" namespace (assembly is ScanditBarcodeCaptureMaui, no dots)
MainPage.xaml contains a <scandit:SparkScanView> element (not <scandit:DataCaptureView>, which is BarcodeCapture's control)
<scandit:SparkScanView> binds its DataCaptureContext property (via {Binding ...} or x:Name + code-behind)
<scandit:SparkScanView> binds its SparkScan property
<scandit:SparkScanView> binds its SparkScanViewSettings property
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions setting SupportedOSPlatformVersion to 24 (or higher) for the Android target
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new SparkScanSettings() is used (not SparkScanSettings.Create(), which does not exist)
new SparkScan(settings) is used (not SparkScan.Create(...), which does not exist)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
sparkScan.BarcodeScanned += handler is wired (or sparkScan.AddListener if using ISparkScanListener)
The page's OnAppearing forwards to this.SparkScanView.OnAppearing() (the MAUI control's own lifecycle method)
The page's OnDisappearing forwards to this.SparkScanView.OnDisappearing()
Permissions.CheckStatusAsync<Permissions.Camera>() or Permissions.RequestAsync<Permissions.Camera>() is called from OnAppearing (or equivalent)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch UI work (not RunOnUiThread, which is Android-only, and not DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync, which is iOS-only)
OnPause/OnResume are NOT called on the SparkScan control (those are the non-MAUI Android binding lifecycle methods)
PrepareScanning/StopScanning are NOT called on the SparkScan control (those are the non-MAUI iOS binding lifecycle methods)

No explicit ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() / ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() call is added in MauiProgram.cs, MainApplication.cs, or AppDelegate.cs (the UseScanditCore/UseScanditBarcode builder handles it for MAUI)

evals.prompt: Add SparkScan to my existing .NET MAUI page. Here's the page: EmptyMainPage.xaml + EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs and MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, show a DisplayAlertAsync with the value.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates SparkScan into the page with QR and Data Matrix enabled, shows a DisplayAlertAsync in the BarcodeScanned handler (dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread), updates MauiProgram.cs with the SparkScan builder chain, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.Qr is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than Qr and DataMatrix are enabled
new SparkScan( is used
The BarcodeScanned event handler reads args.Session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
DisplayAlertAsync (or DisplayAlert) is called with barcode.Data shown
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch the DisplayAlert
MauiProgram.cs calls .UseScanditCore() (no configure lambda) and .UseScanditBarcode(c => c.AddSparkScanView())
Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI SparkScan app, I want to reject barcodes whose value starts with '000' and show an error message in the SparkScan UI for 5 seconds before scanning resumes. Wire this up.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate on the page (or view model), builds a SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback and a SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback(message, resumeCapturingDelay), assigns the delegate to this.SparkScanView.Feedback (or binds via the Feedback bindable property), and returns the error feedback when barcode.Data starts with '000'.

assertions.text: The page (or a dedicated class) implements ISparkScanFeedbackDelegate
GetFeedbackForBarcode(Barcode barcode) is implemented
new SparkScanBarcodeErrorFeedback( is constructed with a message argument and a resumeCapturingDelay argument
resumeCapturingDelay uses TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5) (or TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(5000)) — not a bare integer 5, not a double, not a TimeInterval
new SparkScanBarcodeSuccessFeedback() (or one of its multi-argument constructors) is used for the success case
this.SparkScanView.Feedback is assigned to the delegate instance, OR the <scandit:SparkScanView> Feedback bindable property is bound to the delegate
GetFeedbackForBarcode inspects barcode.Data and returns the error feedback when it starts with '000'
GetFeedbackForBarcode does NOT call MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread or any UI dispatcher (the delegate runs on a background thread)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView bindings, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing) is preserved

evals.prompt: Hide the torch and trigger buttons on my .NET MAUI <scandit:SparkScanView> and add a custom Start button (a MAUI Button) that calls StartScanning() on the control programmatically.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets this.SparkScanView.TorchControlVisible = false and this.SparkScanView.TriggerButtonVisible = false from the code-behind, then wires a MAUI Button.Clicked handler to call this.SparkScanView.StartScanning(). Uses the renamed v7 property TorchControlVisible (not the v6 TorchButtonVisible).

assertions.text: this.SparkScanView.TorchControlVisible = false is set (not TorchButtonVisible, which was removed in v7)
this.SparkScanView.TriggerButtonVisible = false is set
this.SparkScanView.StartScanning() is called from a MAUI Button Clicked event (or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView bindings, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored in my .NET MAUI SparkScan app.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.CodeDuplicateFilter = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) on the SparkScanSettings instance (using TimeSpan, not a bare integer, not a double, not TimeInterval).

assertions.text: settings.CodeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) (not a bare integer 500, not a double 0.5, not TimeInterval)
The value is applied to a SparkScanSettings instance (not BarcodeCaptureSettings)
Either CodeDuplicateFilter is set before new SparkScan(settings), or sparkScan.ApplySettingsAsync(settings) is invoked after the change
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView bindings, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing) is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my .NET MAUI SparkScan app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode via an async HTTP call, and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets this.SparkScan.Enabled = false at the start of the BarcodeScanned handler, awaits the async lookup, and re-enables scanning in a finally block. UI updates are dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread.

assertions.text: this.SparkScan.Enabled = false (or sparkScan.Enabled = false) is set inside the BarcodeScanned handler before the async work
An async/await call or a Task continuation is used to perform the lookup
this.SparkScan.Enabled = true (or sparkScan.Enabled = true) is set after the lookup completes (typically in a finally block)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch UI work after the async lookup (not RunOnUiThread, not DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, SparkScan, SparkScanView bindings, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing) is preserved
